title: XCF Global, Southern Energy, and DevvStream Announce Plan to Pursue Strategic Collaboration to Build Integrated Low-Carbon Fuels Platform and Advance Multi-Pathway SAF Strategy
content: XCF Global, Southern Energy, and DevvStream announced a non-binding tripartite memorandum of understanding (MOU) to jointly explore the potential development of a next-generation low-carbon fuels platform. This collaboration aims to create a unified commercial platform combining fuel supply, logistics, and environmental-attribute value for aviation and industrial customers. The parties intend to advance multiple SAF production pathways, specifically HEFA and next-generation biomass-to-methanol-to-jet. A key focus involves jointly evaluating the future development of "New Rise Louisiana," a HEFA-based SAF facility comparable to XCF's ~40 million gallon New Rise Reno facility. DevvStream is expected to lead the generation, verification, and monetization of environmental assets, including carbon credits and digital MRV solutions. Additionally, the parties plan to explore a long-term offtake framework where XCF could purchase SAF from Southernâ€™s planned $1.4 billion Louisiana facility, while also assessing municipal financing pathways in the region.
author: Analysts Account
